name: From Lebrija to Gibalbín – circular via El Cuervo de Sevilla
name_original: Desde Lebrija a Gibalbín – circular por El Cuervo de Sevilla
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Lebrija are situated in the Lower Guadalquivir region of Andalusia, offering a diverse landscape for cyclists. The area features extensive marshlands, rolling agricultural plains, and foothills of the Sierra Sur. This terrain provides varied cycling experiences, from flat, serene stretches to routes with moderate elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around Lebrija The most popular road cycling route is From Lebrija to Gibalbín – circular via El Cuervo de Sevilla, a 37.0 miles (59.5 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route features well-paved surfaces and offers varied terrain with moderate elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Quiet Country Road – Castillo de Melgarejo loop from Lebrija, a moderate 53.8 miles (86.6 km) path. This route winds through quiet country roads and agricultural lands, providing scenic views of the region. Local road cyclists also love the Lebrija – Quiet Country Road loop from Lebrija, a 39.5 miles (63.6 km) trail leading through rolling plains and agricultural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Road cycling around Lebrija is defined by marshlands, agricultural lands, and foothills, with routes that include historical paths like the Via Augusta. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Lebrija are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 240 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lebrija's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Lebrija?
A: There are 13 road cycling routes around Lebrija, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 4 easy routes, 6 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Lebrija?
A: Lebrija's road cycling routes feature a diverse landscape, including extensive marshlands, rolling agricultural plains, and the foothills of the Sierra Sur. You'll find everything from flat, serene stretches to routes with moderate elevation gains, and even some challenging ascents.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Lebrija offers 4 easy road cycling routes. These routes typically feature flatter terrain through the marshlands and agricultural plains, providing a relaxed cycling experience. For example, the 'Lebrija – Quiet Country Road loop from Lebrija' is a moderate option that winds through rolling plains and agricultural areas.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the Lebrija area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Lebrija has 3 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19321744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcos Dam – Puerta Matrera loop from Lebrija&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 94.3 km with 940m of elevation gain, demanding very good fitness.

Q: Can I find routes that feature historical landmarks or cultural points of interest?
A: Absolutely. Many routes incorporate historical elements. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5389258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Augusta from Cádiz to Seville, Lebrija&lt;/a&gt;, a preserved Roman road, offers a unique cycling experience through history. You can also explore routes that pass by the remains of Lebrija Castle or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fatetar Castle in Espera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes offer stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moorish Castle Lebrija – Mirador de la Peña loop from Lebrija&lt;/a&gt; includes the Peña Viewpoint, which rewards cyclists with unforgettable vistas of Lebrija and its surroundings. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/228834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calle del Práctico Road and Doñana Wetlands View&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses of the significant Doñana Natural Park.

Q: What natural features can I see while road cycling around Lebrija?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You'll cycle through the extensive marshlands of the Lower Guadalquivir, picturesque agricultural lands with olive groves and vineyards, and near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5389255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de Don Melendo&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer views of the Doñana Wetlands, a highly significant natural area known for its biodiversity.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Lebrija?
A: The best seasons for road cycling in Lebrija are typically spring and autumn. During these months, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for ideal cycling conditions. Summers can be very hot, while winters are generally mild but can have more unpredictable weather.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Lebrija?
A: While specific dedicated cyclist parking areas are not detailed, Lebrija, as a cycling-friendly town, generally offers public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival, especially in the town center or near popular route starting points.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Lebrija?
A: The road cycling routes in Lebrija are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from serene marshlands to rolling agricultural plains, and the well-paved surfaces that make for an enjoyable ride. The blend of natural beauty and historical paths like the Via Augusta is also a significant draw.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of Lebrija's road cycling routes are designed as loops, offering convenient circular journeys. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995749318/from-lebrija-to-gibalbin-circular-via-el-cuervo-de-sevilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Lebrija to Gibalbín – circular via El Cuervo de Sevilla&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6880338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quiet Country Road – Castillo de Melgarejo loop from Lebrija&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6880325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lebrija – Quiet Country Road loop from Lebrija&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any permits required for road cycling in Lebrija?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads and established routes around Lebrija. However, if you plan to venture into protected natural areas or private lands, it's always best to check for any specific regulations or access requirements beforehand.
tips: A town that is in two provinces at the same time: Cádiz and Seville. The border is on Avenida de Cádiz.

The location of Lebrija is located between three different environments: that of the Marshes, which occupies most of the territory, that of the Campiña, with dry land and irrigated land close to the Bajo Guadalquivir channel, and that of the Monte, first foothills of the Subbética mountain range.
